text:
Once upon an orange moonlit evening, there was an old owl named Ottermouse.

Ottermouse loved books. Especially children's stories. So one night he snuck into Grandma Willow's attic. Grandma knew nothing. He sat by her favorite shelf quiet as silk.

She read. She read about kittens finding homes.

Then Ottermouse spoke.

"The heart finds what has disappeared".

He hooted.

So he flew off toward dreams.

He wondered about stars.

He felt love.

She didn’t know.

He flew home quiet as whispers. You feel love.

That's strange.

Maybe tomorrow. Maybe forever. Maybe.

You just look.

Some places seem.

Where you're heart.

You could go.

You just do.

You sleep talk.

You want something.

Any something. That's cool.

That's magic.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
